decision.title: DOM renderer, no game framework
decision.summary: Reject KAPLAY despite REQUIREMENTS.xml suggesting it. Two independent previous rounds (Bare v5, Emergent v4) converged on DOM + TypeScript + Vite + iconify-icon after KAPLAY's styled text, missing area(), and scene-arg gotchas bit earlier runs. Going with the consensus is the lower-risk path to a green build and a polished UI in the time budget. The GAD condition's value is in planning discipline and gate tracking, not in re-litigating framework choice.
decision.impact: Renderer = direct DOM + CSS. Icons = iconify-icon web component + @iconify-json/game-icons. No canvas. No game loop — event-driven scene router. Simpler mental model per scene.

decision.title: Ingenuity clause for forge combinations
decision.summary: Forge supports authored combos AND unknown combos. Any unknown 2-rune combo produces a valid spell via an "ingenuity recipe": rune types fold together deterministically (e.g. fire+wind -> firestorm DoT). Prevents softlock and rewards experimentation per the v4 pressure-oriented requirements.
decision.impact: Forge is never a dead-end. All 5*4 ordered pairs yield a spell.

decision.title: Scene router = top-level string, not SPA
decision.summary: A single #app div, a renderScene() dispatcher by state.scene enum. Simple, debuggable, and avoids framework overhead. Each scene module exports a render(root) function that clears and repopulates.
decision.impact: Easy to add new scenes; localStorage save-per-tick is trivial.

decision.title: Pressure gate G4: resource + counterplay + encounter
decision.summary: Three pressure mechanics, not just two: (A) mana and crystal pools constrain casting/forging, (B) enemy resistances and reflect, (C) authored elites with unique rules. At least (B) and (C) interact with the forge by making crafted status/DoT/summon spells strictly better than the starter magic missile.
